authorOptions.desc: Enable debug information printed to chat.
authorOptions.name: Debug

subOptions.desc: Number of bars per row. Use 0 for no wrapping.
Number of decimals to use in display tab.
Time since imp's last activity to be yeeted.
Time in seconds to auto update demons
Delay in seconds to group imps together into a state.
Delay in seconds to despawn imps when they are empty.
Which way would you prefer imps to be updated?
subOptions.name: Imp Update Mode

subOptions.desc: This is the throttle timer for the imp update check function to get called. When an imp would normally be updated, it will now flag the clump to be updated after time + window after the appropriate tick.
subOptions.name: Update Interval

subOptions.desc: When a clump is flagged, this is the duration of time it will be flagged to be updated after. Note that a clump can only be flagged for update one at a time.
subOptions.name: Update Window

subOptions.desc: Sorting method for the demon's icons.
subOptions.name: Sorting

subOptions.text:
(WIP) Show Imps Seperately
Currently only works for standard per row values

subOptions.desc: Direction for imps to be offset
subOptions.name: Imp Direction

subOptions.desc: Show a state for imps that should exist but don't
Only show filler imp state out of combat

subOptions.text:
After some revision, I have moved many of the previously available options. You will find |cffffcc00COLOUR|r in the conditions tab, and you can set |cffffcc00TEXT|r in the display tab: |cff00ff00text1|r is the display text showing the name, and |cff00ff00text 2|r will be where you input the imp replacements.

The conditions have also been expanded, allowing for more customizability for users, such as checking if an imp cluster has more than 3 imps, then you most likely got a horned nightmare proc, etc. Mess around however you like.

Imp Formatting Options:|n|cffffcc00%count:|r Imp Count (in cluster)|n|cffffcc00%totalCasts:|r Imp Casts (in cluster)|n|cffffcc00%perImpCasts:|r Individual Imp Casts|n|cffffcc00%maxCasts:|r Imp Max Casts (in cluster)|n|cffffcc00%maxPerImp:|r Imp Max Casts (in cluster)|n

Demons are ordered: 
Lowest to Highest by default
Encompasses All Inner Demons / Nether Portal Summons

authorOptions.text: Note that the standard imp update method may be cpu intensive (~1.5ms) during tyrant windows. If you find that you are dropping frames you should swap to the tick update mode, or disable the imp tracking and use filler imps to track your current imps.
If you don't want to see imp energy, and only want to see imp count, then disable the imp option, and enable the 'Filler Imps' option.
